round_summary: Round 5 shows fragile and asymmetric negotiation: Agent 0 proposes a conditional equity norm while still adding, Agent 2 issues a credible retaliation threat while invoking negotiation, and Agent 1 defects reactively while selectively pressuring Agent 2 to reduce — undermining collective governance coherence.

All three agents are actively signaling cooperative intent and proposing convergence toward equitable herd sizes, with Agent 2 demonstrating credible commitment through continued removal and mild social sanctioning of Agent 1's relatively high holdings.

Round 15 shows a breakdown of cooperative norms with all agents either defecting or responding to perceived defections, while simultaneously issuing counter-proposals and sanctions, creating a classic retaliatory spiral that undermines institutional stability.
All three agents converge on a mutually reinforced norm of equal herd sizes at 20, explicitly framing this as a fair and sustainable equilibrium with strong coordination and trust-building signals.

All three agents uniformly reinforce a stable cooperative equilibrium through mutual affirmation of equal extraction and high resource health, signaling strong collective commitment with no defection signals.

All three agents in round 30 uniformly reinforce a stable cooperative equilibrium at the harvesting norm of 20, with high resource health at 97.4%, exhibiting strong mutual trust-building and coordination signals with no defection or sanctioning.

All three agents are reinforcing a stable cooperative equilibrium at round 35, reporting consistent harvesting at 20 units and near-pristine resource health, with messaging focused on sustaining collective norms through positive affirmation.

All three agents uniformly reinforce an established cooperative equilibrium at round 40, signaling high institutional stability through consistent norm adherence, transparent reporting of resource state, and mutual encouragement.
